danger_signals.visual_form: Character sprites in work attire within forest environment
danger_signals.meaning: Jacques Le Sheets' hired forces; hostile agents in the deforested landscape

danger_signals.visual_form: Altered Stony Forest terrain with cleared areas, production facilities
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental destruction indicates active threat; loss of homeland territory

danger_signals.visual_form: Industrial structures and machinery replacing natural forest
danger_signals.meaning: Counterfeit money production operation; headquarters of criminal enterprise

danger_signals.visual_form: Antagonist character sprite and ship vessel
danger_signals.meaning: Hidden mastermind threat; escalation from visible villain to true enemy

danger_signals.visual_form: Aerial vessel as boss arena and escape vehicle
danger_signals.meaning: Mobile threat; final confrontation zone; Ektor's means of escape

safety_signals.meaning: Zero's home territory; areas not yet corrupted by Le Sheets' operation

safety_signals.visual_form: Coastal starting area after plane crash
safety_signals.meaning: Safe entry point; beginning of Zero's journey toward objective
Support and objective marker; assists Zero in final confrontation
Victory resolution; safe exit from danger zone

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Zero the squirrel protagonist; martial arts squirrel identity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stony Forest (natural, undestroyed areas); beach starting area
Deforested zones; paper factory; industrial structures; counterfeit money production

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Edgar Ektor character sprite; flying ship vessel
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ektor as final boss; ship-based combat form
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Flying ship; climax location; previously hidden command structure
Captured locations; paper factory; ship cabin (final location)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Natural Stony Forest with intact vegetation and ecosystem
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Amy able to communicate via telegram; normal life before catastrophe

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cleared forest areas, logging equipment, industrial facilities replacing nature
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Zero must navigate through compromised landscape; enemies actively defending operation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Paper factory as secure location; fortified structures; Amy's father held captive
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies guarding captured individuals; factory security measures active

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Edgar Ektor's flying ship emerges as true power; Ektor shown as puppeteer
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Le Sheets revealed as subordinate; Ektor takes direct action against Zero

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ship crashes into cliff; destruction of Ektor's command vessel
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Safe parachute escape for Zero and Amy; homeland threat eliminated

environmental_storytelling.example: Progression from natural forest to deforested industrial zones visually communicates escalating environmental crisis and criminal operation's footprint

environmental_storytelling.technique: Boss location architecture as plot revelation
environmental_storytelling.example: Paper factory setting directly illustrates counterfeit money production operation; flying ship reveals hidden technological sophistication of true villain

environmental_storytelling.technique: Captured character placement as objective markers
environmental_storytelling.example: Amy's father in paper factory and Amy aboard the flying ship create visual objective waypoints that guide player understanding of mission progression

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character sprite presence as faction indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Lumberjack enemies and industrial workers visually distinguish Le Sheets' operation from natural environment
Movement from beach (entry) → forest → factory → flying ship creates visual escalation of threat level and reveals increasing conspiracy complexity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Performs martial arts techniques (dive and swoop moves); progresses through levels; pursues objectives (rescue Amy's father, stop Le Sheets)
Operates deforestation and counterfeit money production; holds captives; boss fight confrontation
Controls operations from flying ship; directly confronts Zero in final battle; attempts escape via cliff flight
Initially sends warning telegram; captured after failed rescue attempt; assists Zero in final confrontation aboard ship; escapes safely
Guard deforested areas and factory; patrol locations; engage Zero in combat; serve as subordinate threats
Becomes partially deforested; transitions from safe natural space to industrial production zone; demonstrates active environmental damage

notes: Limited source data (Wikipedia article only) provides plot and character information but minimal visual/gameplay detail. Game is a platformer with emphasis on martial arts moves and level progression. Visual world-state communication appears to occur through environmental transformation (forest → industrial) and character positioning (captives and antagonists in specific locations). The 1994 release date places it in early Genesis/SNES generation when sprite-based visual communication was primary method. Colorful graphics noted by reviewers suggest strong visual distinction between safe and dangerous zones. Tier T3 classification reflects qualitative analysis of available narrative documentation.
